pythonTasks.prompt: #Write a print statement to display the text Hello World.
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
SEND
“Hello World”
TO DISPLAY

pythonTasks.prompt: #Write an input statement asking the user their name and store their answer in a variable
pythonTasks.pseudocode: RECEIVE name FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING

pythonTasks.prompt: #Write an input statement asking the user their age and store their answer in a variable
pythonTasks.pseudocode: RECEIVE age FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER

pythonTasks.prompt: #Use concatenation and print the sentence 'Your name is........and you are........years old pulling the user's name and age from the variables.
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
RECEIVE name FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
RECEIVE age FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
SEND [
“Your name is ”
& name &
“ and you are ”
& age &
“ years old.”
] TO DISPLAY

pythonTasks.prompt: #Write an input statement asking the user to enter the price of a product (remember could be a decimal)
pythonTasks.pseudocode: RECEIVE price FROM KEYBOARD AS REAL

pythonTasks.prompt: #Write an if statement to check if their age is less than or equal to 18. If it is then tell the user they can get a child ticket. If not then they can purchase an adult ticket.
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
RECEIVE age FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
IF age <= 18 THEN
SEND
“You can get a child ticket”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE
SEND
“You can get an adult ticket”
TO DISPLAY
END IF

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Write an if statement that checks if the user’s favorite subject is Computing. If it’s Computing display a message saying, Great choice! If it’s not then display a message saying Hmm, you sure?!?
#Next write an if statement to check if their favorite subject is not Computing. If it’s not Computing then display a message saying Hmm, you sure?!? If it is Computing (since it’s not
not
Computing), then display a message saying, Great choice!
#Both tasks are doing the same thing, just in reverse!
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
Method 1:
RECEIVE subject FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
IF subject IS EQUAL TO
“Computing”
THEN
SEND
“Great choice!”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE
SEND
“Hmm, you sure?!?”
TO DISPLAY
END IF
—----------------------------------------------------------------
Method 2:
RECEIVE subject FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
IF subject DOES NOT EQUAL
“Computing”
THEN
SEND
“Hmm, you sure?!?”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE
SEND
“Great choice!”
TO DISPLAY
END IF

pythonTasks.prompt: #Set the cost of a ticket to 25. Ask how many tickets the user wants to buy. Calculate and print the total cost in a sentence.
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
SET ticketPrice TO 25
RECEIVE ticketAmount FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
SET totalCost TO ticketAmount * ticketPrice
SEND [
“Your total is ”
& totalCost] TO DISPLAY

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Ask the user their mark in the Chemistry test
#If they got more than or equal to 70 then tell them they got an A
#If they got between 60-69 then tell them they got a B
#If they got between 50-59 then tell them they got a C
#If they got less than 50 then tell them better luck next time
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
RECEIVE chemistryMark FROM KEYBOARD AS REAL
IF chemistryMark >= 70 THEN
SEND
“You got an A!”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE IF chemistryMark >= 60 THEN
SEND
“You got a B!”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE IF chemistryMark >= 50 THEN
SEND
“You got a C!”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE
SEND
“Better luck next time”
TO DISPLAY
END IF

pythonTasks.prompt: #Use a for loop to ask the user the names of their five friends
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
REPEAT 5 TIMES
RECEIVE friendName FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
END REPEAT

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Ask the user how many students sat the exam.
#Use a for loop to ask for each student's grade (A, B, C….)
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
RECEIVE studentNum FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
REPEAT studentNum TIMES
RECEIVE studentGrade FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
END REPEAT

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Ask the user how many items they bought when out shopping
#Keep a running total within a loop to calculate the total price
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
SET total TO 0
RECEIVE itemsBought FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
REPEAT itemsBought TIMES
RECEIVE itemPrice FROM KEYBOARD AS REAL
SET total TO total + itemPrice
END REPEAT
SEND total TO DISPLAY

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Ask the user how many tickets they want to buy.
#Keep a running total within a loop to calculate the total price. You will need to ask the user each time if it's a child ticket (5 pounds) or adult ticket (10 pounds).
#(nested if within a for loop)
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
SET total TO 0
SET childTicket TO 5
SET adultTicket TO 10
RECEIVE ticketAmount FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
REPEAT ticketAmount TIMES
RECEIVE ticketType FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
IF ticketType IS EQUAL TO
“child”
THEN
SET total TO total + childTicket
ELSE IF ticketType IS EQUAL TO
“adult”
THEN
SET total TO total + adultTicket
END IF
END REPEAT
SEND total TO DISPLAY

pythonTasks.prompt: #Use input validation when asking the user to enter their Chemistry test result between 0-100
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
Method 1:
RECEIVE chemistryResult FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
WHILE chemistryResult < 0 OR chemistryResult > 100 DO
SEND
“Error invalid mark please enter again.”
TO DISPLAY
RECEIVE chemistryResult FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
END WHILE
Method 2:
RECEIVE chemistryResult FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
WHILE NOT(chemistryResult >= 0 AND chemistryResult <= 100) DO
SEND
“Error invalid mark please enter again.”
TO DISPLAY
RECEIVE chemistryResult FROM KEYBOARD AS INTEGER
END WHILE
While not valid
While not(what is valid)

pythonTasks.prompt: #Ask the user to choose a movie genre: 'A' for Action, 'C' for Comedy, or 'D' for Drama. If they enter anything else, keep asking until they provide a valid input.
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
Method 1:
RECEIVE movieGenre FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
WHILE movieGenre NOT
“A”
AND movieGenre NOT
“B”
AND movieGenre NOT
“C”
DO
SEND “Error! Invalid movie genre, please enter again” TO DISPLAY
RECEIVE movieGenre FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
END WHILE
Method 2:
RECEIVE movieGenre FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
WHILE NOT(movieGenre =
“A”
OR movieGenre =
“B”
OR movieGenre =
“C”)
DO
SEND “Error! Invalid movie genre, please enter again” TO DISPLAY
RECEIVE movieGenre FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
END WHILE

fruits

,

,

]

pythonTasks.prompt:
#In a single line of code and using the array to specify the element, display "maths".
subjects= ["computing", "chemistry", "English", "maths", "Chinese", "PE"]
pythonTasks.pseudocode:

subjects TO [“computing”, “chemistry”, “English”, “maths”, “Chinese”, “PE”]

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Create an array that stores the pocket money from each day one week (7 elements)
#Keep a running total within a loop adding up the money from each entry in the array.

#Create an empty array with five empty spots that will store song names for a playlist..

#Using a new for loop print the songs in the playlist one at a time

#Scenario: You are creating a new membership ID for an exclusive club.

#Use the length function to check the length of the ID and prompt the user until they enter a valid one.
#Write a program that generates a random prize number between 0 and 5 and displays a fun message based on the prize number.

#Write a program that generates a random exercise from an array and displays it to the user.
#Exercise surprise options: "10 push-ups", "20 sit-ups", "15 squats", "30-second plank", "20 jumping jacks", "10 burpees", "15 lunges", "30-second high knees", "15 tricep dips", "20 mountain climbers"
pythonTasks.answer: It’s too small to see clearly so here’s a larger example by dropping some of the code to the next line.

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Ask the user how many movies they want to add to the list.
#Use a loop to let the user enter the names of each movie.
#Store all the movie names in a list.
#Randomly select one movie from the list and display it to the user.
#Write a program that asks for the price of 5 items, calculates the average, and rounds the result to 2dp.
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
SET total TO 0
REPEAT 5 TIMES
RECEIVE price FROM KEYBOARD AS REAL
SET total TO total + price
END REPEAT
SET average TO total/5
SEND round(average, 2) TO DISPLAY

pythonTasks.prompt:
#Scenario: Your school is planning an activities week, and students are encouraged to submit unique activity ideas.
#Each activity idea must be between 5 and 30 characters long. Your task is to collect and validate these activity ideas and print out the list one by one.

#
A library has a system to check if a book is available for borrowing. Each book is represented by a variable that stores whether it is available (True) or borrowed (False).
#Write code to check if a book is available.
#If the book is available, update its status to borrowed (False) and display a message: "You have successfully borrowed the book."
#
If the book is not available, display a message: "Sorry, the book is currently not available."
pythonTasks.pseudocode:
RECEIVE bookName FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
SET bookAvailable TO TRUE
RECEIVE borrowDecision FROM KEYBOARD AS STRING
IF borrowDecision =
“yes”
THEN
IF bookAvailable = TRUE THEN
SET bookAvailable TO FALSE
SEND [
“You have successfully borrowed: ”
& bookName &
“.”
] TO DISPLAY
ELSE
SEND [
“Sorry, ”
& bookName &
“ is currently not available.”
] TO DISPLAY
END IF
ELSE IF borrowDecision =
“no”
THEN
SEND
“Ok, maybe next time.”
TO DISPLAY
ELSE
SEND
“Error”
TO DISPLAY
END IF

sqlTasks.prompt: Output all the details from the table.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT *
FROM Golfer;
Back
to
Question

sqlTasks.prompt: Output the full names of all the golfers.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT forename, surname
FROM Golfer;

sqlTasks.prompt: Output all the details of the senior golfers.
Output the Scottish Golf Numbers and club names of the junior golfers.
Output the forename and club of the golfers with a handicap of less than 6.
Output the movie name and certificate level for movies with a length of at least 110
Output the movie name and movie star for movies with a length of no more than 120.
Output the full names for those students whose first test scored over 50
Output all the details for those students whose second test scored 90.
Output all the details for those students who scored at least 50 in both tests.
Output all the details for those students who scored over 50 in at least one test.

This is a table for balloon orders.
Output all the details for the orders that requested personalise (field type = boolean). Order the results by collection date.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT *
FROM Order
WHERE personalise = True
ORDER BY collection ASC;

sqlTasks.prompt: This is a table for balloon orders. Output the order ID and price for the orders that requested a red balloon. Order the results by price in descending order.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT orderID, price
FROM Order
WHERE colour = “Red”
ORDER BY price DESC;

sqlTasks.prompt: This is a table for balloon orders. Output the order collection date and type of balloon for the orders that are of type Star. Order the results by order ID.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT collection, type
FROM Order
WHERE type = “Star”
ORDER BY OrderID ASC;

sqlTasks.prompt: This is a table for balloon orders. Output the order ID, type of balloon, and collection date. Order the results by type in descending order.
This is a run result from the table called contract. Write the SQL query that would produce this. It has two orders but no where. Exam questions like this normally have two orders, look carefully!
Output all the details from the table. Order by sessionBlock in descending order, then by time in ascending order.
Output the names and studio IDs from the table for those characters that have over 10 appearances.  Order by name in ascending order and then by studio ID in descending order.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT name, studioID
FROM Character
WHERE appearances > 10
ORDER BY name ASC, studioID DESC;

sqlTasks.prompt: Output the character name and studioName from the tables.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT Character.name, Studio.studioName
FROM
Character, Studio
WHERE
Character
.studioID =
Studio
.studioID;

sqlTasks.prompt: Create an SQL query that uses an Equi join to display a list of each team along with the specific equipment they use. The result should show the team name and equipment name.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT [Teams Table].TeamName, [Equipment Table].EquipName
FROM [Teams Table], [Equipment Table]
WHERE [Teams Table].TeamID = [Equipment Table].TeamID

sqlTasks.prompt: Output the artist name and label name from the tables.
Write an SQL query to output the forename, surname, age, and score from the tables for the members of Dundee Golf club. Order the results by handicap in descending order.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT Golfer.forename, Golfer.surname, Golfer.age, Result.score
FROM Golfer, Result
WHERE Golfer. club = “Dundee Golf Club” AND Golfer.scotGolfNo = Result.scotGolfNo
ORDER BY Golfer.handicap DESC;

sqlTasks.prompt: Write an SQL query to output the forename, surname, and match type from the tables for the matches with a score of over 70. Order the results by match type and then by year in descending order.
sqlTasks.answer:
SELECT Golfer.forename, Golfer.surname, Result.matchType
FROM Golfer, Result
WHERE Result.score > 70 AND Golfer.scotGolfNo = Result.scotGolfNo
ORDER BY Result.matchType ASC, Result.year DESC;

sqlTasks.prompt: Add a new book with ID B003, "The Great Gatsby" by F. Scott Fitzgerald, published in 1925, and categorised under the genre "Fiction."
sqlTasks.answer:
INSERT INTO Books
VALUES (“B003”, “The Great Gatsby”, “F.Scott Fitzgerald”, 1960, “Fiction”);

sqlTasks.prompt: Add a new artist to the table. Add Billie Eilis, ID A003, with genre Pop.
sqlTasks.answer:
INSERT INTO Artists
VALUES (“A003”, “Billie Eilis”, “Pop”);

sqlTasks.prompt: Add a new swimmer with swimming ID S003, "Emma McKeon," to the database. You have her TeamID (T102), but her stroke specialty is currently undecided so leave that field blank.
sqlTasks.answer:
INSERT INTO Swimmers (SwimmerID, SwimmerName, TeamID)
VALUES (“S003”, “Emma McKeon”, “T102”);

sqlTasks.prompt:
Add a new artist to the table.
Add Ed Sheeran, ID A004, without specifying the genre.
sqlTasks.answer:
INSERT INTO Artists (ArtistID, ArtistName)
VALUES (“A004”, “Ed Sheeran”);

sqlTasks.prompt: The class originally scheduled in Room 101 needs to be moved to Room 202 because the assigned teacher is absent. Update the table using an SQL update statement.
sqlTasks.answer:
UPDATE ClassSchedule
SET RoomNumber = “Room 202”
WHERE ClassID = C001;
Tip: The
structure of an UPDATE statement for a single record is:
UPDATE tablename
SET fieldName = new value
WHERE primaryKeyFieldName = primaryKeyValue;

sqlTasks.prompt: The Olympic canteen needs to adjust its inventory after a popular athlete, known as "the Muffin Man" on social media, bought an unusually large quantity of chocolate muffins, causing a spike in demand and subsequently depleting the stock faster than anticipated. Write an SQL UPDATE statement to decrease the quantity of "Chocolate Muffin" by 100 units due to high demand influenced by the athlete's purchases.

Several ski competition events have had their dates changed because there wasn't enough fresh snow. Write an SQL UPDATE statement to change the event dates for all the competitions originally planned for the 12/02/2024 to the later date of 25/02/2024.
sqlTasks.answer:
UPDATE SkiCompetitions
SET EventDate = #25/02/2024#
WHERE EventDate = #12/02/2024#;
Tip:
Notice this time we didn’t identify the fields to change using the primary key because we wanted to change multiple records. The structure is:
UPDATE tableName
SET fieldname = newValue
WHERE fieldname = oldValue;

sqlTasks.prompt: Instructor SUP01 is leaving, and all of their classes (Beginner Waves and Sunset Paddle) will now be taught by Instructor SUP03. Update the instructor for all classes originally taught by Instructor SUP01 to Instructor SUP03 and adjust the MaxParticipants for these classes to 10.
The "Soccer" event scheduled for 18/06/2024 has been cancelled, and you need to remove it from the SportsEvents table below. Create an SQL Delete statement.
sqlTasks.answer:
DELETE FROM SportsEvents
WHERE EventID = “EV002”;
Tip:
We use DELETE Statements to delete whole rows/records. IMPORTANT: You must specify exactly which record to delete or it will delete all the records! Unless you actually want to delete everything. It’s a good idea to use the primary key here to make sure we only delete that one row/record.

sqlTasks.prompt:
The activity "Stroll Through Duthie Park" has been temporarily removed due to renovations. Write a SQL DELETE statement to remove the activity "Stroll Through Duthie Park" from t
he AberdeenActivities table.
sqlTasks.answer:
DELETE FROM AberdeenActivities
WHERE ActivityID = “A002”;
